verbatim_text: Well, first of all, you observe that for the state-of-the-art models, right, if you train on regular ImageNet and test on a snowy day, you have automatic performance score. But if you were to cheat, you know, if you were to remove the distribution shift, right, you just train and test both on a snowy day, then there's no fault. So would it be nice if we had some training data on a snowy day? Well, in fact, we have one. That's this test instance over here. The only thing is it is this side of the boundary. So you've got to be willing to use that test instance to update your model. But this input is just an unlabeled input, right? It has no label. That's why it's a test instance. So how do you generate data for this unlabeled test instance to do the test time training? Well, in 2019, my idea was to use self-supervised learning. And let me just give you a short illustration. So yeah, you want to do object recognition.
